title: Cross-track mechanism summary and 149 K gap update

master_candidate_table.dynamic_stability: STABLE (but AF instability in inner planes)
master_candidate_table.synthetic_accessibility: LOW (difficult to grow, phase purity issues)

master_candidate_table.dynamic_stability: LIKELY STABLE (analog to La3Ni2O7, needs verification)
PREDICTED STABLE (no imaginary modes in model)
master_candidate_table.synthetic_accessibility: LOW (no demonstrated synthesis, requires precise MBE)

Hg_cuprates.note: Well-established from 30 years of cuprate literature. Phonon-mediated Eliashberg captures only the low-Tc tail.

bilayer_nickelates.note: Higher phonon fraction than cuprates due to stronger c-axis phonon coupling to dz2 sigma-bonding. Still insufficient alone.

hybrid_superlattice.note: Interface charge transfer effects not captured by isotropic Eliashberg.

gap_update_149K.honest_assessment: No candidate computed in Phases 28-30 exceeds the existing Hg1223 phonon-only benchmark of 31.4 K. The 149 K gap (300 K - 151 K experimental) remains unchanged. Within the phonon-only Eliashberg framework, no oxide material can approach 300 K because (a) phonon frequencies in oxides are limited to omega_log ~ 250-350 K, (b) lambda_phonon for layered oxides is limited to 0.7-1.5, and (c) these together give a Tc ceiling of ~30-40 K for the phonon channel alone.

DMFT_plus_Eliashberg.description: Dynamical mean-field theory provides the correct correlated electronic structure. Combined with Eliashberg on the DMFT Green's function, this captures both phonon and spin-fluctuation pairing.
DMFT_plus_Eliashberg.expected_improvement: Could predict Tc ~ 80-150 K for Hg1223 (based on literature: Kitatani et al. PRL 2022 for cuprates, Worm et al. 2024 for nickelates)
DMFT_plus_Eliashberg.computational_cost: 100-1000 node-hours per material (accessible with modern HPC)
DMFT_plus_Eliashberg.status: Established method, implemented in codes like w2dynamics + TRIQS, AbinitioDGA

RPA_spin_fluctuation.description: Random phase approximation for magnetic susceptibility, used as input to spin-fluctuation Eliashberg. Simpler than DMFT.
RPA_spin_fluctuation.expected_improvement: Could predict Tc ~ 50-100 K (less accurate than DMFT but captures spin-fluctuation channel)
RPA_spin_fluctuation.status: Widely used for cuprates and nickelates (Scalapino, RMP 2012)

Hg1223_PQP_reproduction.description: Independent reproduction of the 151 K pressure-quench result
Hg1223_PQP_reproduction.value: Confirms the 151 K benchmark is real and reproducible

Sm3Ni2O7_SLAO_film.description: Grow Sm3Ni2O7 film on SLAO substrate, measure Tc
Sm3Ni2O7_SLAO_film.value: Tests whether RE substitution + strain cooperatively enhance nickelate Tc

pressurized_nickelate_zero_resist.description: Establish zero-resistance Tc for La3Ni2O7 under pressure (currently ambiguous onset vs zero-resist)
pressurized_nickelate_zero_resist.value: Resolves the onset/zero-resist ambiguity that dominates the phonon fraction uncertainty

HG-04.evidence: Hg multilayer mechanism quantified: AF inner-plane competition, plane-resolved lambda, phonon fraction ~22%

NI-04.evidence: Nickelate mechanism quantified: dz2 orbital enhancement, strain-lambda correlation, phonon fraction ~45%

VALD-03.evidence: 149 K gap explicitly stated as UNCHANGED. Best phonon-only Tc = 31.4 K (Hg1223). No new candidate exceeds this.
